title: Oishika Gupta | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:

333 W Wacker Dr 15th Floor
Fragomen in Chicago, IL

Fragomen in Chicago, IL
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
333 W Wacker Dr 15th Floor
Oishika is valued by clients for her ability to focus on the essentials of immigration challenges—risk assessment and management, compliance management and clear, effective communication.
She brings clarity to notoriously opaque and confusing immigration processes by building policy management frameworks that identify and call out potential difficulties so that clients can manage them ahead of time with minimum difficulty. She applies sophisticated data and trend analyses, industry benchmarking, employee feedback and the latest intelligence from within consulates and regulatory bodies to build sound policies that are as effective as possible while remaining flexible in the face of regulatory or economic change.
Clients value her ability to apply a wide-angle perspective to their non-immigration business processes and policies, through which she singles out unexpected threats to compliance and other immigration considerations. An effective manager, Oishika adopts this high-level strategic focus while her team ensures that program details are executed flawlessly.
Throughout her career, Oishika has worked with clients from a range of industries, including technology, consulting, manufacturing, healthcare, management consulting and biopharmaceuticals. She got her start at Fragomen as a core member of the firm’s Foreign Consular Practice (FCP) team in India and became a Partner in 2021. She has been practicing in the firm’s Chicago office since 2011.
She maintains a robust pro bono practice rooted in her background in human rights and refugee law. Early in her career, she worked with Amnesty International and the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees and assisted the senior attorney on the Supreme Court of India in high-profile litigation cases.
More recently, she has volunteered with Child Rights and You (CRY) —a nonprofit international human rights organization—where she spearheaded an anti-child labor campaign. She assists with individual pro bono casework through Fragomen’s pro bono practice, including Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) clients and asylum matters. She is also a proud contributor to Fragomen’s diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
Oishika speaks Bengal, English and Hindi.

- Georgetown University Law Center, Master of Law, with distinction , 2009
- The West Bengal National University of Juridical Sciences, Bachelor of Law, 2008
Bar Admissions: New York
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Chambers USA, 2021 to 2023
Global Law Teaching Fellow, Georgetown University, 2008 to 2009
Research Assistant, O’Neill Institute for National and Global Health, Georgetown University, 2008 to 2009

### Professional accomplishments
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association (AILA)
Member, New York Bar Association
Committee Member, AILA National Pro-Bono Services Committee
Committee Member, AILA Chicago Volunteer Legal Services (CVLS) Committee
Member and DEI Chair, ProVisors
Coordinated H-1B cap filing process for several high-volume IT consulting firms.
Contributed to the development of a comprehensive nonimmigrant visa strategy document for a leading IT consulting firm.
Worked with large IT consulting clients to set up and coordinate their overall immigration strategy and nonimmigrant and immigrant visa practices.
Successfully processed several complex O-1 petitions.
Contributed to the development of comprehensive consular practices, including mock interview strategies, with a focus on India and Manila.
Developed strategies and processes for Batch PERM filings for high-volume IT consulting companies.
Collaborated with clients to develop their overall immigration strategies post acquisition and merger.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
